title: acer dasycarpum var. dissectum
intro: Acer dasycarpum var. dissectum, also known as the Cutleaf Paperbark Maple, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to China and Japan, characterized by its distinctive dissected leaves and reddish-brown bark. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids. The health benefits of Acer dasycarpum var. dissectum include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and support the immune system, as well as its potential to prevent cancer and cardiovascular disease. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in combination with other herbs to enhance their medicinal effects.
